metadata.framing_note: Metabolic-as-signalling: enzymes are activators of their product metabolite (sign=+1), competing sinks are inhibitors (sign=-1). FLASH-P geometric-mean / bounded-inverse math is applied to a flux problem by treating enzyme expression level as signal strength. Steady-state algebraic prediction reports qualitative increased/decreased/unchanged lycopene titer under perturbations.
metadata.build_summary: Iteration 1. Selected rate-limiting MEP (Dxs, Idi) and MVA (AtoB/HMGS/HMGR/MVK/MVD) enzymes; kept metabolite pools (G3P, Pyruvate, Acetyl_CoA, Mevalonate, IPP, DMAPP, GPP, FPP, GGPP, Phytoene, NADPH, ATP, ROS) as explicit cascade nodes; IspB encoded as FPP competing sink (sign=-1); GPP/FPP/GGPP -> MVK product-feedback inhibition; ROS<->Lycopene self-limiting feedback with KatE protection; RpoS (Crl+/Hnr-) regulator layer; Almgs->PlsB->Lycopene membrane storage. Pruned from network (kept in curated_edges.json as documented biology): Dxr, IspD/E/F/G/H, PMK (to keep IPP activators <=7); OxyR/KatG (KatG has no outgoing edge to ROS); AppY, CRP, SodA, DgkA, Plsc, Chassis_K12, Medium_Richness, TpiA, TalB, AccABCD, EutD, FdhF, AlphaKGDH, Lpp/NlpI/MlaE/TolA/WaaC/WaaF/BamB, Y-gene knockout-screen targets, FabI_stress, UspE, YggE (phenotype-activator overload or single-edge peripherals). Also dropped Dxs->G3P(-) and Dxs->Pyruvate(-) substrate-drain edges (Wang 2009 MAGE non-monotonic dose-response) because they cancel Dxs OE signal boost at the modest OE levels used in most validation tests; the biology stays in curated_edges.json.
residual_literature_gaps_handled: RpoS->Dxs (Alper 2005) vs Bongers 2015 ROS reinterpretation: adopted ROS-route (KatE, ROS<->Lycopene feedback) as dominant chassis mechanism; no RpoS->Dxs edge used.
Yoon 2009 / Alper 2006 / Jin 2008 / Zhao 2013 paywall papers: no edges fabricated; their abstract-level claims are already represented via Wang 2020 and Su 2023 reviews in curated_edges.
Neurosporene-proxy tests (T093-T098): magnitude is proxy for lycopene; VALIDATOR/REFINEMENT concern, not BUILDER.

nodes.description: Primary carbon source entering PTS then EMP glycolysis; feeds G3P and Pyruvate pools that are the MEP substrate.
Alternative carbon source; phosphorylated to G3P via glycerol kinase, boosting the G3P pool relative to pyruvate.
Alternative carbon source feeding PPP then EMP; supplies G3P and pyruvate to MEP.
Lac-operon inducer for trc/lac/T7-driven Dxs, Idi, CrtE, CrtB, CrtI cassettes. Dose-response titration input.
pBAD-system inducer for MEP and IspA cassettes.
Effective plasmid dosage (pUC~500, pBR322~20, pACYC~15) gating heterologous enzyme expression ceiling.
Aerobic vs microaerobic growth — low O2 de-represses ArcA/FNR, limits ROS, altering carotenoid yield.
30 degC is typically optimal for heterologous carotenoid synthesis; 37 degC lowers soluble enzyme expression and pigment stability.
DXS substrate pool fed by glycolysis (Pgi) and the ED shortcut (Edd/Eda); drained by Zwf (PPP) and GapA (lower glycolysis).
DXS second substrate and Acetyl-CoA precursor. Net pyruvate level is the dominant flux knob for MEP (Farmer 2000).
TCA and MVA precursor. Produced by AceE (PDH); drained by Ppc (anaplerosis) and Pta (acetate).
Signal intermediate upstream of NRI (NtrC phosphorylation proxy).
Reducing cofactor used by HMGR (MVA) and CrtI (four-step desaturation). Produced by Gnd (PPP oxidative) and drained by GdhA.
Phosphate-donor cofactor for MVK (MVA kinase step). Treated as a supply source for cofactor gating.
MVA intermediate pool; substrate for MVK/PMK/MVD chain to IPP.
Universal C5 isoprenoid monomer produced by both MEP (via Dxs/Idi) and MVA (via MVK/MVD). Substrate for IspA.
C5 allylic isomer of IPP produced by Idi and MEP terminal (IspH). Partner of IPP for IspA-catalysed GPP/FPP synthesis.
C10 prenyl intermediate from IspA. Feedback inhibits MVK; at high levels is toxic to lycopene titer (Chen 2022).
C15 branch-point metabolite. Feeds CrtE (productive) and IspB (ubiquinone, competing sink).
C20 precursor; condensed by CrtB to phytoene.
Colourless C40 carotenoid; CrtI desaturates to lycopene through four steps.
Superoxide / peroxide / singlet-oxygen pool. Oxidatively degrades lycopene; quenched by lycopene itself (Bongers 2015).
Glucose-6-P to fructose-6-P isomerase — gateway into EMP glycolysis. Knockout redirects flux through PPP and ED.
PPP entry enzyme. Delta-zwf increases EMP flux and raises lycopene ~130% (Sung 2013).
Lower-glycolysis enzyme draining G3P to 1,3-bisPG. RBS-down gapA raises G3P pool for MEP (Farmer 2000).
PEP -> pyruvate kinase. Together with PykF sets pyruvate:G3P balance for MEP.
Major pyruvate kinase. Delta-pykF can redirect PEP away from pyruvate.
PEP -> OAA anaplerotic branch. Diverts PEP from pyruvate/Acetyl-CoA supply.
Pyruvate -> PEP. Farmer 2000 classic: Pps OE rebalances pyruvate and G3P pools to improve isoprenoid precursor ratio.
Entner-Doudoroff dehydratase. With Eda gives 1:1 G3P:pyruvate stoichiometry matching DXS substrate needs (Li 2015).
Entner-Doudoroff aldolase. KDPG -> pyruvate + G3P, feeding MEP directly.
PPP oxidative step producing Ru5P and NADPH. NADPH supplier for HMGR/CrtI cofactor demand.
Drains NADPH during ammonium assimilation. Delta-gdhA increases NADPH availability (Alper 2006 screen).
PDH E1 subunit converting pyruvate -> Acetyl-CoA. Transcriptionally repressed by ArcA and FNR under anaerobic conditions.
Drains Acetyl-CoA to acetyl-P (overflow acetate). Delta-pta can redirect Acetyl-CoA into MVA.
Drains pyruvate to lactate under microaerobic conditions. Minor sink.
MEP rate-limiting enzyme condensing pyruvate + G3P to DXP. The most-perturbed target in lycopene engineering (~3x OE boost).
Equilibrates IPP and DMAPP pools. Secondary rate-limiting for MEP; Idi OE on top of Dxs OE gives combined ~16x lycopene.
MVA upper: 2 Acetyl-CoA -> acetoacetyl-CoA. Source of MVA cassette.
Acetoacetyl-CoA + Acetyl-CoA -> HMG-CoA.
HMG-CoA + 2 NADPH -> mevalonate. Truncated tHMGR variant used in many OE strains for improved soluble expression.
Mevalonate + ATP -> mevalonate-5-P. Major MVA downstream bottleneck; feedback inhibited by GPP/FPP/GGPP (Chen 2022).
Terminal MVA enzyme producing IPP from mevalonate-5-PP.
Condenses IPP+DMAPP -> GPP, then IPP+GPP -> FPP. Supplies the FPP branch point.
Essential competing sink: FPP -> octaprenyl-PP -> ubiquinone/menaquinone. RBS-down IspB gives modest lycopene gain (cannot KO).
Heterologous: FPP + IPP -> GGPP. Plasmid-encoded, IPTG-inducible.
Heterologous: two GGPP -> phytoene.
Heterologous four-step desaturase: phytoene -> lycopene. NADPH cofactor dependence.
Sigma-S stress-response factor. Activates KatE and contributes to carotenoid accumulation (Alper 2005; Bongers 2015 reinterprets as ROS route).
Post-translationally stabilises RpoS holoenzyme assembly.
Adaptor targeting RpoS for ClpXP degradation. Delta-hnr increases RpoS and lycopene.
Two-component response regulator repressed by high oxygen; represses aerobic TCA including AceE.
Oxygen-sensing [4Fe-4S] TF; represses aerobic metabolism genes including AceE under anoxia.
[2Fe-2S] sensor of oxidative stress that activates SoxS.
AraC-family TF induced by SoxR; upregulates Zwf (PPP oxidative) for NADPH demand.
LacI-family TF; activates gluconeogenic genes including pps under glycolytic-substrate limitation.
Nitrogen-regulatory response regulator phosphorylated in response to Acetyl-P signalling; induces Pps and Idi in Alper 2005 screen.
Sigma-S-induced hydrogen peroxide catalase. OE reduces oxidative lycopene degradation and collapses strain-level titer variation (Bongers 2015).
Membrane-phospholipid biosynthesis. Increases phospholipid reservoir for hydrophobic lycopene storage (Hu 2018).
Heterologous monoglucosyl-DAG synthase; synergises with PlsB to expand membrane storage 1.32x (Hu 2018).
Accumulated lycopene mass per cell biomass (mg/g DCW) or per volume. Metabolic-flux endpoint.

edges.mechanism: Glucose enters PTS then Pgi-driven EMP glycolysis, supplying G3P as a DXS substrate.

evidence.title: Metabolic Engineering Escherichia coli for the Production of Lycopene
evidence.authors: Wang Z, Sun J, Yang Q, Yang J
evidence.evidence_sentence: glyceraldehyde 3-phosphate (G3P) and pyruvate are condensed to form 1-deoxy-d-xylulose-5-phosphate (DXP) by DXP synthase (DXS)
evidence.claim: G3P is a direct precursor feeding the MEP pathway via DXS

edges.mechanism: Glucose glycolysis terminates in pyruvate, the second DXS substrate.

evidence.title: Metabolic Engineering Escherichia coli for the Production of Lycopene
evidence.authors: Wang Z, Sun J, Yang Q, Yang J
evidence.evidence_sentence: deletion of the zwf gene encoding glucose-6-phosphate dehydrogenase (G6PD)...resulted in the increased carbon fluxes in the Embden-Meyerhof-Parnas (EMP) pathway and indirectly led to an increase in lycopene production
evidence.claim: Glucose flows through EMP glycolysis to pyruvate, a precursor of DXP via DXS

edges.mechanism: Glycerol is phosphorylated then oxidised to DHAP/G3P, enriching G3P relative to pyruvate.

evidence.title: Metabolic Engineering Escherichia coli for the Production of Lycopene
evidence.authors: Wang Z, Sun J, Yang Q, Yang J
evidence.evidence_sentence: Glycerol is a viable carbon source for beta-carotene and lycopene production, as it increases glyceraldehyde 3-phosphate and pyruvate, which are imperative intermediates in central carbon metabolism extension to the MEP pathway
evidence.claim: Glycerol carbon source increases G3P availability relative to glucose

edges.mechanism: Xylose feeds the pentose-phosphate shunt producing F6P and G3P for MEP.

evidence.title: Metabolic Engineering Escherichia coli for the Production of Lycopene
evidence.authors: Wang Z, Sun J, Yang Q, Yang J
evidence.evidence_sentence: Xylose is another viable carbon source that feeds glycolysis via xylose isomerase and xylulokinase, supplying pyruvate and G3P to the MEP pathway
evidence.claim: Xylose supplies both MEP precursors via upper glycolysis

edges.mechanism: Xylose catabolism terminates in pyruvate via EMP.

evidence.title: Metabolic Engineering Escherichia coli for the Production of Lycopene
evidence.authors: Wang Z, Sun J, Yang Q, Yang J
evidence.evidence_sentence: Xylose is another viable carbon source that feeds glycolysis via xylose isomerase and xylulokinase, supplying pyruvate and G3P to the MEP pathway
evidence.claim: Xylose supplies both MEP precursors via upper glycolysis

edges.mechanism: Pgi (phosphoglucose isomerase) routes G6P into the EMP branch feeding G3P.

evidence.title: Improving Product Yields on D-Glucose in Escherichia coli via Knockout of pgi and zwf
evidence.authors: Archer FS, Jerez BP, Prather KL
evidence.evidence_sentence: Deletion of pgi and zwf was found to eliminate catabolite repression as well as the ability of E. coli to consume glucose for biomass formation, and the yield from glucose of the bioproduct D-glucaric acid was significantly increased in a delta-pgi delta-zwf strain
evidence.claim: Pgi controls glucose entry into glycolysis; its deletion reroutes flux through PPP

edges.mechanism: Pgi-gated EMP flux terminates in pyruvate.

evidence.title: Improving Product Yields on D-Glucose via pgi and zwf Knockout
evidence.authors: Archer FS, Jerez BP, Prather KL
evidence.evidence_sentence: Deletion of pgi and zwf was found to eliminate catabolite repression as well as the ability of E. coli to consume glucose for biomass formation
evidence.claim: Pgi sustains glycolytic flux to pyruvate

edges.mechanism: Zwf diverts G6P into the oxidative PPP, reducing G3P supply to DXS; Δzwf raises lycopene ~130%.

evidence.title: Advances in the Discovery and Engineering of Gene Targets for Carotenoid Biosynthesis in Recombinant Strains
evidence.authors: Su B, Deng MR, Zhu H
evidence.evidence_sentence: knockout of the zwf gene, significantly enhanced lycopene production by over 130%
evidence.claim: Zwf competes with glycolysis for G6P; deletion increases MEP precursor flux

evidence.title: Lycopene production in recombinant strains of Escherichia coli is improved by knockout of the central carbon metabolism gene coding for glucose-6-phosphate dehydrogenase
evidence.authors: Sung JH, Yoon SH, Lee JH, Kim SW
evidence.evidence_sentence: deletion of the zwf gene encoding glucose-6-phosphate dehydrogenase...resulted in the increased carbon fluxes in the Embden-Meyerhof-Parnas pathway and indirectly led to an increase in lycopene production
evidence.claim: Î”zwf redirects carbon from PPP to glycolysis, enhancing lycopene

edges.mechanism: GapA (GAPDH) drains G3P into 1,3-bisPG in lower glycolysis.

evidence.title: Improving lycopene production in Escherichia coli by engineering metabolic control
evidence.authors: Farmer WR, Liao JC
evidence.evidence_sentence: the distribution between pyruvate and glyceraldehyde 3-phosphate (G3P), the originating precursors of the isoprenoid pathway, is a major factor that can limit isoprenoid production yields in E. coli, with alterations in the central metabolism that redirect flux from pyruvate back to G3P enhance lycopene production
evidence.claim: Reducing G3P-consuming flux (via gapA) boosts isoprenoid precursor pool

edges.mechanism: PykA (pyruvate kinase II) converts PEP to pyruvate.

evidence.title: Metabolic Engineering Escherichia coli for the Production of Lycopene
evidence.authors: Wang Z, Sun J, Yang Q, Yang J
evidence.evidence_sentence: deleting pyruvate kinases-I and -II (Pyk-I and-II)...lycopene production was significantly increased
evidence.claim: pykA knockout reroutes PEP away from excess pyruvate, improving precursor balance

edges.mechanism: PykF (major pyruvate kinase) converts PEP to pyruvate.

evidence.title: Metabolic Engineering Escherichia coli for the Production of Lycopene
evidence.authors: Wang Z, Sun J, Yang Q, Yang J
evidence.evidence_sentence: deleting pyruvate kinases-I and -II (Pyk-I and-II)...lycopene production was significantly increased
evidence.claim: Combined pykA+pykF knockout improves lycopene titer

edges.mechanism: Pps (PEP synthase) drains pyruvate back to PEP, reducing pyruvate pool for DXS.

evidence.title: Advances in the Discovery and Engineering of Gene Targets for Carotenoid Biosynthesis in Recombinant Strains
evidence.authors: Su B, Deng MR, Zhu H
evidence.evidence_sentence: overexpressing phosphoenolpyruvate (PEP) synthase (PPS)...achieved a 250% increase in lycopene production by...diverting flow from pyruvate to G3P
evidence.claim: Pps overexpression pulls pyruvate back to PEP, rebalancing toward G3P

edges.mechanism: Pps-driven pyruvate→PEP redirection ultimately raises upstream G3P availability (Farmer 2000 flux rebalance).

evidence.title: Improving lycopene production in Escherichia coli by engineering metabolic control
evidence.authors: Farmer WR, Liao JC
evidence.evidence_sentence: alterations in the central metabolism that redirect flux from pyruvate back to G3P enhance lycopene production
evidence.claim: Pps-mediated pyruvate-to-PEP shift supports G3P replenishment

evidence.title: Improving lycopene production in E. coli by engineering metabolic control
evidence.authors: Farmer WR, Liao JC
evidence.evidence_sentence: the distribution between pyruvate and glyceraldehyde 3-phosphate (G3P), the originating precursors of the isoprenoid pathway, is a major factor that can limit isoprenoid production yields in E. coli, with alterations in the central metabolism that redirect flux from pyruvate back to G3P enhance lycopene production
evidence.claim: Pyruvate:G3P balance is the dominant flux control; PpsA OE (pyruvate->PEP) rebalances and raises lycopene

edges.mechanism: Edd (6-PG dehydratase) produces KDPG that Eda splits into G3P + pyruvate (Entner-Doudoroff).

evidence.title: Engineering the E. coli ribulose-5-phosphate pathway for carotenoid production via the Entner-Doudoroff shortcut
evidence.authors: Li C, Ying L, Zhang S, Chen N, Liu W, Tao Y
evidence.evidence_sentence: The ED pathway is a shortcut for G3P and pyruvate generation. It produces the two MEP precursors in equal amounts via a four-step reaction from G6P.
evidence.claim: Edd + Eda generate G3P and pyruvate in 1:1 stoichiometry from G6P, directly feeding MEP

edges.mechanism: ED pathway via Edd supplies pyruvate stoichiometrically with G3P, matching DXS 1:1 substrate need.

evidence.title: Engineering the E. coli ribulose-5-phosphate pathway for carotenoid production via the Entner-Doudoroff shortcut
evidence.authors: Li C, Ying L, Zhang S, Chen N, Liu W, Tao Y
evidence.evidence_sentence: The ED pathway is a shortcut for G3P and pyruvate generation. It produces the two MEP precursors in equal amounts via a four-step reaction from G6P.
evidence.claim: Edd + Eda generate G3P and pyruvate in 1:1 stoichiometry from G6P, directly feeding MEP

edges.mechanism: Eda (KDPG aldolase) splits KDPG into G3P + pyruvate.

evidence.title: Engineering the E. coli ribulose-5-phosphate pathway for carotenoid production via the Entner-Doudoroff shortcut
evidence.authors: Li C, Ying L, Zhang S, Chen N, Liu W, Tao Y
evidence.evidence_sentence: The ED pathway is a shortcut for G3P and pyruvate generation. It produces the two MEP precursors in equal amounts via a four-step reaction from G6P.
evidence.claim: Edd + Eda generate G3P and pyruvate in 1:1 stoichiometry from G6P, directly feeding MEP

edges.mechanism: Eda-generated pyruvate matches the ED 1:1 stoichiometry required by DXS.

evidence.title: Engineering the E. coli ribulose-5-phosphate pathway for carotenoid production via the Entner-Doudoroff shortcut
evidence.authors: Li C, Ying L, Zhang S, Chen N, Liu W, Tao Y
evidence.evidence_sentence: The ED pathway is a shortcut for G3P and pyruvate generation. It produces the two MEP precursors in equal amounts via a four-step reaction from G6P.
evidence.claim: Edd + Eda generate G3P and pyruvate in 1:1 stoichiometry from G6P, directly feeding MEP

edges.mechanism: Ldh (lactate dehydrogenase) drains pyruvate to lactate under microaerobic growth.

evidence.title: Metabolic Engineering Escherichia coli for the Production of Lycopene
evidence.authors: Wang Z, Sun J, Yang Q, Yang J
evidence.evidence_sentence: lactate production pathways
evidence.claim: ldh contributes to pyruvate drain under fermentative conditions

edges.mechanism: AceE (PDH E1) is the pyruvate→Acetyl-CoA entry point feeding TCA and MVA.

evidence.title: Metabolic Engineering Escherichia coli for the Production of Lycopene
evidence.authors: Wang Z, Sun J, Yang Q, Yang J
evidence.evidence_sentence: knockout of aceE would presumably improve carbon flux to pyruvate and formate, and further deletion of fdhF
evidence.claim: aceE knockout preserves pyruvate pool for DXS

edges.mechanism: Pta drains Acetyl-CoA via acetyl-P to overflow acetate.

evidence.title: Metabolic Engineering Escherichia coli for the Production of Lycopene
evidence.authors: Wang Z, Sun J, Yang Q, Yang J
evidence.evidence_sentence: deletion of the acetate and lactate production pathways...elimination of the acetate pathway
evidence.claim: Pta/AckA deletion reduces acetate overflow

edges.mechanism: Ppc (PEP carboxylase) diverts PEP toward OAA, reducing the upstream supply feeding AceE and Acetyl-CoA.

evidence.title: Metabolic Engineering Escherichia coli for the Production of Lycopene
evidence.authors: Wang Z, Sun J, Yang Q, Yang J
evidence.evidence_sentence: overexpressing Ppc and Pck to introduce the bypass pathway between PEP and pyruvate via the oxaloacetate and TCA cycle
evidence.claim: Ppc overexpression reroutes PEP through oxaloacetate, reducing acetyl-CoA overflow

edges.mechanism: Glucose-driven glycolysis produces acetyl-P as a signalling metabolite for the NtrC branch.

evidence.title: Metabolic Engineering Escherichia coli for the Production of Lycopene
evidence.authors: Wang Z, Sun J, Yang Q, Yang J
evidence.evidence_sentence: ACP, an indicator of glucose flux
evidence.claim: Acetyl-P increases with glucose flux

edges.mechanism: Acetyl-P phosphorylates NRI (NtrC) in the absence of the dedicated kinase.

evidence.title: Metabolic Engineering Escherichia coli for the Production of Lycopene
evidence.authors: Wang Z, Sun J, Yang Q, Yang J
evidence.evidence_sentence: NRI...capable of sensing the level of acetyl phosphate (ACP), an indicator of glucose flux
evidence.claim: Acetyl-P activates NRI via the Ntr TCS

edges.mechanism: NRI-P activates pps promoter (Alper 2005 sigma-S-family screen).

evidence.title: Improving lycopene production in Escherichia coli by engineering metabolic control
evidence.authors: Farmer WR, Liao JC
evidence.evidence_sentence: recruited and altered the Ntr regulon (a global regulatory system in Escherichia coli) to control the engineered lycopene biosynthesis pathway
evidence.claim: NRI (Ntr regulon) controls engineered glnAp2-driven pps and idi expression

edges.mechanism: NRI-P induces idi transcription in the same regulon.

evidence.title: Improving lycopene production in Escherichia coli by engineering metabolic control
evidence.authors: Farmer WR, Liao JC
evidence.evidence_sentence: activating the expression of pathway genes (pps and idi) upon high acetyl phosphate levels
evidence.claim: NRI induces idi via glnAp2 in engineered circuit

edges.mechanism: Cra (FruR) activates pps under glycolytic-substrate limitation.

evidence.title: Improving lycopene production in Escherichia coli by engineering metabolic control
evidence.authors: Farmer WR, Liao JC
evidence.evidence_sentence: designed a controller that monitors the overflow of acetyl phosphate in the glycolytic pathway by an Ntr regulation system controlled by the glnAp2 promoter, with the system activating the expression of pathway genes (pps and idi)
evidence.claim: PPS expression is metabolically controlled; Cra also binds ppsA promoter

edges.mechanism: High oxygen oxidises the ArcB sensor, dephosphorylating ArcA and relieving its repression of aerobic genes.

evidence.title: Metabolic Engineering Escherichia coli for the Production of Lycopene
evidence.authors: Wang Z, Sun J, Yang Q, Yang J
evidence.evidence_sentence: presumably improve carbon flux to pyruvate and formate
evidence.claim: Oxygen level modulates ArcA activity (canonical E. coli physiology)

edges.mechanism: O2 oxidises the FNR [4Fe-4S] cluster, disassembling the active dimer.

evidence.title: Metabolic Engineering Escherichia coli for the Production of Lycopene
evidence.authors: Wang Z, Sun J, Yang Q, Yang J
evidence.evidence_sentence: anaerobic energy metabolism
evidence.claim: FNR oxygen sensing: high O2 inactivates FNR

edges.mechanism: Phosphorylated ArcA represses the aceEF-lpd operon under anaerobiosis.

evidence.title: Metabolic Engineering Escherichia coli for the Production of Lycopene
evidence.authors: Wang Z, Sun J, Yang Q, Yang J
evidence.evidence_sentence: knockout of aceE would presumably improve carbon flux to pyruvate and formate
evidence.claim: ArcA represses aceE under microaerobic conditions (well established E. coli physiology)

edges.mechanism: FNR represses aceE under anaerobiosis.

evidence.title: Metabolic Engineering Escherichia coli for the Production of Lycopene
evidence.authors: Wang Z, Sun J, Yang Q, Yang J
evidence.evidence_sentence: knockout of aceE would presumably improve carbon flux to pyruvate
evidence.claim: FNR represses aceE under anaerobic conditions (canonical E. coli physiology)

edges.mechanism: Aerobic respiration generates superoxide/peroxide through oxidative phosphorylation side-reactions.

evidence.title: Systems analysis of MEP pathway flux in E. coli: insights into oxidative stress
evidence.authors: Bongers JG, et al.
evidence.evidence_sentence: proteins involved in aerobic respiration and the TCA cycle were increased when compared to Seq+
evidence.claim: Higher O2/aerobic metabolism increases ROS

edges.mechanism: Gnd (6-PG dehydrogenase) produces NADPH during the PPP oxidative branch.

evidence.title: Engineering the E. coli ribulose-5-phosphate pathway for carotenoid production via the Entner-Doudoroff shortcut
evidence.authors: Li C, Ying L, Zhang S, Chen N, Liu W, Tao Y
evidence.evidence_sentence: knockout of the gnd gene (6-phosphogluconate dehydrogenase) in the Delta-pgi background significantly decreased neurosporene production
evidence.claim: Gnd feeds PPP-oxidative flux; in Delta-pgi its deletion starves the PPP-dependent NADPH supply needed for MEP

edges.mechanism: GdhA uses NADPH for glutamate biosynthesis, competing with carotenoid cofactor demand.

evidence.title: Metabolic Engineering Escherichia coli for the Production of Lycopene
evidence.authors: Wang Z, Sun J, Yang Q, Yang J
evidence.evidence_sentence: gdhA deletion could increase the availability of NADPH
evidence.claim: gdhA knockout increases NADPH pool for isoprenoid biosynthesis

edges.mechanism: Acetyl-CoA is the primary substrate of the MVA upper pathway (AtoB/HMGS/HMGR).

evidence.title: Metabolic Engineering Escherichia coli for the Production of Lycopene
evidence.authors: Wang Z, Sun J, Yang Q, Yang J
evidence.evidence_sentence: MVA pathway initiates with acetyl-CoA, which is converted into MVA through three reactions, catalyzed by acetoacetyl-CoA thiolase (ACCT), 3-hydroxy-3-methylglutaryl-CoA (HMG-CoA) synthase (HMGS)
evidence.claim: Acetyl-CoA is the upstream precursor for the MVA pathway

edges.mechanism: AtoB condenses two Acetyl-CoA to acetoacetyl-CoA; first committed MVA step.

evidence.title: Rationally optimized generation of integrated Escherichia coli with stable and high yield lycopene biosynthesis from heterologous mevalonate (MVA) and lycopene expression pathways
evidence.authors: Hussain MH, et al.
evidence.evidence_sentence: mvaE and mvaS...catalyzes the conversion from acetyl-CoA to mevalonic acid
evidence.claim: mvaE (atoB+HMGR fusion) activity drives mevalonate production from acetyl-CoA

evidence.title: Reconstitution of the MVA Pathway with BSL-1 Enzymes for Enhanced Lycopene Production in E. coli
evidence.authors: Kang MK, Lee S, Park Y, and colleagues
evidence.evidence_sentence: pSCS3-pLYC produced 77 mg/l lycopene, 8.6-fold higher than pSTV28-pLYC control and 97% higher than pSNA-pLYC
evidence.claim: E. saccharolyticus mvaE/mvaS + S. cerevisiae erg12/19/8 (pSCS3) outperforms E. faecalis + S. pneumoniae by ~97% at 48 h batch

edges.mechanism: HMGS condenses acetoacetyl-CoA + Acetyl-CoA to HMG-CoA.

evidence.title: Metabolic Engineering Escherichia coli for the Production of Lycopene
evidence.authors: Wang Z, Sun J, Yang Q, Yang J
evidence.evidence_sentence: catalyzed by acetoacetyl-CoA thiolase (ACCT), 3-hydroxy-3-methylglutaryl-CoA (HMG-CoA) synthase (HMGS) and HMG-CoA reductase (HMGR)
evidence.claim: HMGS catalyses HMG-CoA formation on the MVA route to mevalonate

edges.mechanism: HMGR reduces HMG-CoA to mevalonate using two NADPH — the major MVA rate-limiting step.

evidence.title: Advances in the Discovery and Engineering of Gene Targets for Carotenoid Biosynthesis in Recombinant Strains
evidence.authors: Su B, Deng MR, Zhu H
evidence.evidence_sentence: overexpressed tHMGR from X. dendrorhous, a truncated form of HMGR...2.2-fold enhancement in beta-carotene production
evidence.claim: HMGR (tHMGR) overexpression is rate-limiting for MVA flux

edges.mechanism: NADPH is a strict cofactor for HMGR reduction; NADPH depletion limits MVA flux.

evidence.title: Rationally optimized integrated E. coli MVA lycopene
evidence.authors: Hussain MH, et al.
evidence.evidence_sentence: MVA pathway needs 2 NADPH for the production of mevalonate
evidence.claim: NADPH required for HMGR activity

edges.mechanism: Dxs-driven MEP pathway terminates in IPP (via IspH); the rate-limiting contribution to the IPP pool.

evidence.title: Advances in the Discovery and Engineering of Gene Targets for Carotenoid Biosynthesis in Recombinant Strains
evidence.authors: Su B, Deng MR, Zhu H
evidence.evidence_sentence: the step that limits the rate of the MEP pathway...overexpression of dxs in E. coli carotenoid production was enhanced 3.5-fold
evidence.claim: DXS is the main rate-limiting enzyme of the MEP pathway

evidence.title: Metabolic Engineering Escherichia coli for the Production of Lycopene
evidence.authors: Wang Z, Sun J, Yang Q, Yang J
evidence.evidence_sentence: Overexpression of dxs yielded 1.33 mg/g DCW
evidence.claim: Quantitative DXS OE benefit for lycopene

edges.mechanism: G3P is the direct substrate contribution feeding MEP flux into IPP (pool model).

evidence.title: Metabolic Engineering Escherichia coli for the Production of Lycopene
evidence.authors: Wang Z, Sun J, Yang Q, Yang J
evidence.evidence_sentence: glyceraldehyde 3-phosphate (G3P) and pyruvate are condensed to form 1-deoxy-d-xylulose-5-phosphate (DXP) by DXP synthase (DXS)
evidence.claim: G3P feeds MEP to IPP

edges.mechanism: Pyruvate is the second substrate contribution feeding MEP flux into IPP.

evidence.title: Metabolic Engineering Escherichia coli for the Production of Lycopene
evidence.authors: Wang Z, Sun J, Yang Q, Yang J
evidence.evidence_sentence: glyceraldehyde 3-phosphate (G3P) and pyruvate are condensed to form 1-deoxy-d-xylulose-5-phosphate (DXP) by DXP synthase (DXS)
evidence.claim: Pyruvate feeds MEP to IPP

edges.mechanism: Idi equilibrates DMAPP back to IPP, feeding the IPP pool under DMAPP excess.

evidence.title: Metabolic Engineering E. coli for Lycopene
evidence.authors: Wang Z, Sun J, Yang Q, Yang J
evidence.evidence_sentence: isopentenyl-diphosphate isomerase (IDI) catalyzes the isomerization of IPP to DMAPP
evidence.claim: Idi equilibrates IPP/DMAPP pools

edges.mechanism: Mevalonate pool feeds IPP via MVK/PMK/MVD enzymatic chain.

evidence.title: Metabolic Engineering Escherichia coli for the Production of Lycopene
evidence.authors: Wang Z, Sun J, Yang Q, Yang J
evidence.evidence_sentence: MVA is converted to mevalonate-5-phosphate (MVAP) catalyzed by mevalonate kinase (MK)...two reactions successively catalyzed by MVAP kinase (PMK) and MVAPP decarboxylase (MDD)
evidence.claim: Mevalonate feeds IPP via MVK/PMK/MVD

edges.mechanism: MVK-driven MVA downstream contributes to the IPP pool; rate-limiting MVA kinase step.

evidence.title: Directed evolution of mevalonate kinase in Escherichia coli by random mutagenesis for improved lycopene
evidence.authors: Tan X, et al.
evidence.evidence_sentence: Mevalonate kinase (MK) is one of the rate-limiting enzymes in the lycopene synthetic pathway
evidence.claim: MVK catalyses the first downstream phosphorylation step in MVA; rate-limiting for lycopene

edges.mechanism: MVD (terminal MVA enzyme) produces IPP from mevalonate-5-PP.

evidence.title: Metabolic Engineering Escherichia coli for the Production of Lycopene
evidence.authors: Wang Z, Sun J, Yang Q, Yang J
evidence.evidence_sentence: MVAPP decarboxylase (MDD)
evidence.claim: MVD/MDD finalises the lower MVA conversion to IPP

edges.mechanism: Idi equilibrates IPP to DMAPP — a critical supply arm because IspA needs DMAPP to start C10/C15 chain elongation.

evidence.title: Advances in the Discovery and Engineering of Gene Targets for Carotenoid Biosynthesis in Recombinant Strains
evidence.authors: Su B, Deng MR, Zhu H
evidence.evidence_sentence: IDI is a crucial enzyme...mutant IDI exhibited a 1.8-fold increase in lycopene production
evidence.claim: Idi equilibrates IPP and DMAPP; directed-evolution variants boost lycopene

edges.mechanism: IPP pool is isomerised to DMAPP (pool model contribution).

evidence.title: Metabolic Engineering Escherichia coli for the Production of Lycopene
evidence.authors: Wang Z, Sun J, Yang Q, Yang J
evidence.evidence_sentence: isopentenyl-diphosphate isomerase (IDI) catalyzes the isomerization of IPP to DMAPP
evidence.claim: Metabolite pool coupling: IPP <-> DMAPP

edges.mechanism: IPTG induces trc/T7-driven dxs expression cassettes.

evidence.title: Metabolic Engineering Escherichia coli for the Production of Lycopene
evidence.authors: Wang Z, Sun J, Yang Q, Yang J
evidence.evidence_sentence: highest lycopene yield (22 mg/L) was reached with the arabinose-inducible promoter on a medium-copy plasmid pBAD24 in the E. coli XL1-Blue strain
evidence.claim: Inducer-controlled expression of dxs (IPTG or arabinose) modulates MEP flux

edges.mechanism: IPTG induces idi cassettes co-expressed with dxs.

evidence.title: Metabolic Engineering Escherichia coli for the Production of Lycopene
evidence.authors: Wang Z, Sun J, Yang Q, Yang J
evidence.evidence_sentence: RBS libraries...leading to an increase in lycopene yield by 32%
evidence.claim: Induction of idi via IPTG-responsive promoters is standard practice

edges.mechanism: IPTG induces crtE from the carotenoid operon.

evidence.title: Engineering the lycopene synthetic pathway in E. coli by comparison of the carotenoid genes of Pantoea agglomerans and Pantoea ananatis
evidence.authors: Lee JH, Kim SW, Lee PC
evidence.evidence_sentence: E. coli harboring the P. agglomerans crt genes produced 27 mg/l of lycopene in 2YT medium without IPTG induction, which was twofold higher than that produced by E. coli harboring the P. ananatis crt genes (12 mg/l lycopene) with 0.1 mM IPTG induction
evidence.claim: IPTG controls crt cassette expression when cloned under lac-regulated promoters

edges.mechanism: IPTG induces crtB from the carotenoid operon.

evidence.title: Engineering the lycopene synthetic pathway in E. coli by comparison of the carotenoid genes of Pantoea agglomerans and Pantoea ananatis
evidence.authors: Lee JH, Kim SW, Lee PC
evidence.evidence_sentence: E. coli harboring the P. agglomerans crt genes produced 27 mg/l of lycopene in 2YT medium without IPTG induction
evidence.claim: Inducer drives crt cassette expression including crtB

edges.mechanism: IPTG induces crtI from the carotenoid operon.

evidence.title: Engineering the lycopene synthetic pathway in E. coli by comparison of the carotenoid genes of Pantoea agglomerans and Pantoea ananatis
evidence.authors: Lee JH, Kim SW, Lee PC
evidence.evidence_sentence: 0.1 mM IPTG induction
evidence.claim: Inducer drives crt cassette expression including crtI

edges.mechanism: pBAD-driven dxs cassettes respond dose-dependently to arabinose.

evidence.title: Evaluation of Various Escherichia coli Strains for Enhanced Lycopene Production
evidence.authors: Ren J, Shen J, Thai TD, Kim M, Lee SH, Lim W, Na D
evidence.evidence_sentence: dxs, dxr, ispA, and idi from E. coli...expressed under the PBAD promoter regulated by AraC protein and arabinose
evidence.claim: Arabinose induces PBAD-driven dxs OE plasmids

edges.mechanism: pBAD-driven idi cassettes respond to arabinose.

evidence.title: Evaluation of Various Escherichia coli Strains for Enhanced Lycopene Production
evidence.authors: Ren J, Shen J, Thai TD, Kim M, Lee SH, Lim W, Na D
evidence.evidence_sentence: dxs, dxr, ispA, and idi from E. coli...expressed under the PBAD promoter regulated by AraC protein and arabinose
evidence.claim: Arabinose induces idi via PBAD

edges.mechanism: pBAD-driven ispA cassettes respond to arabinose in MEP-extension engineering.

evidence.title: Evaluation of Various Escherichia coli Strains for Enhanced Lycopene Production
evidence.authors: Ren J, Shen J, Thai TD, Kim M, Lee SH, Lim W, Na D
evidence.evidence_sentence: dxs, dxr, ispA, and idi from E. coli...expressed under the PBAD promoter regulated by AraC protein and arabinose
evidence.claim: Arabinose induces ispA via PBAD

edges.mechanism: Higher plasmid copy raises crtE expression ceiling (pUC > pBR322 > pACYC).

evidence.title: Rationally optimized generation of integrated E. coli with stable MVA lycopene
evidence.authors: Hussain MH, et al.
evidence.evidence_sentence: one copy of MVA upper pathway, two copies of MVA lower pathway, and three copies of lycopene expression pathway was comprehended as the best performer
evidence.claim: Gene dosage (copy number) tunes crt expression and titer

edges.mechanism: Higher plasmid copy raises crtB expression ceiling.

evidence.title: Rationally optimized generation of integrated E. coli with stable MVA lycopene
evidence.authors: Hussain MH, et al.
evidence.evidence_sentence: three copies of lycopene expression pathway was comprehended as the best performer
evidence.claim: Copy number tunes crtB dosage

edges.mechanism: Higher plasmid copy raises crtI expression ceiling.

evidence.title: Rationally optimized generation of integrated E. coli with stable MVA lycopene
evidence.authors: Hussain MH, et al.
evidence.evidence_sentence: three copies of lycopene expression pathway was comprehended as the best performer
evidence.claim: Copy number tunes crtI dosage

edges.mechanism: Idi placed on tunable plasmids shows dosage-dependent lycopene response (Cheng 2022).

evidence.title: Fine-tuning the downstream MVA pathway by systematic RBS engineering for improved lycopene production
evidence.authors: Cheng T, Wang L, Sun C, Xie C
evidence.evidence_sentence: IDI activity appeared to be critical for a high lycopene pathway... all the strains showed stronger TIR of RBS-IDI
evidence.claim: IDI RBS strength dominates downstream-MVA optimum; weakening IDI depresses all tested strains

evidence.title: Fine-tuning the downstream MVA pathway by systematic RBS engineering for improved lycopene production
evidence.authors: Cheng T, Wang L, Sun C, Xie C
evidence.evidence_sentence: As the replication origin of the midstream module changed from p15A to pSC101 with reducing the copy number, lycopene production further improved
evidence.claim: Lower plasmid copy for midstream module prevents over-flux; LM22 reaches 219.7 mg/g DCW (4.6x baseline)

edges.mechanism: MVK expression scales with plasmid copy; too high causes GPP/FPP toxicity (Cheng 2022).

evidence.title: Fine-tuning the downstream MVA pathway by systematic RBS engineering for improved lycopene production
evidence.authors: Cheng T, Wang L, Sun C, Xie C
evidence.evidence_sentence: As the replication origin of the midstream module changed from p15A to pSC101 with reducing the copy number, lycopene production further improved
evidence.claim: Lower plasmid copy for midstream module prevents over-flux; LM22 reaches 219.7 mg/g DCW (4.6x baseline)

edges.mechanism: ATP is a strict cofactor for MVK (mevalonate kinase).

evidence.title: Metabolic Engineering Escherichia coli for the Production of Lycopene
evidence.authors: Wang Z, Sun J, Yang Q, Yang J
evidence.evidence_sentence: IPP and DMAPP...need NADPH and ATP
evidence.claim: ATP required for lower MVA phosphorylation and decarboxylation

edges.mechanism: IspA condenses IPP+GPP → FPP (C15).

evidence.title: Metabolic Engineering Escherichia coli for the Production of Lycopene
evidence.authors: Wang Z, Sun J, Yang Q, Yang J
evidence.evidence_sentence: farnesyl pyrophosphate (FPP) is formed from GPP catalyzed by FPP synthase (IspA)
evidence.claim: IspA is FPP synthase, the main E. coli prenyltransferase to FPP

edges.mechanism: IspA first condenses IPP+DMAPP → GPP (C10) before FPP.

evidence.title: Metabolic Engineering Escherichia coli for the Production of Lycopene
evidence.authors: Wang Z, Sun J, Yang Q, Yang J
evidence.evidence_sentence: condensation reaction between the two intermediates occurs with the formation of geranyl diphosphate (GPP)
evidence.claim: IspA forms GPP from DMAPP+IPP

edges.mechanism: IPP substrate pool feeds FPP synthesis (pool contribution).

evidence.title: Metabolic Engineering Escherichia coli for the Production of Lycopene
evidence.authors: Wang Z, Sun J, Yang Q, Yang J
evidence.evidence_sentence: FPP is then catalyzed by geranylgeranyl diphosphate synthase (CrtE), phytoene synthase (CrtB), and phytoene desaturase (CrtI) to successively form GGPP, phytoene, and finally lycopene
evidence.claim: FPP pool depends on IPP

edges.mechanism: DMAPP substrate pool feeds GPP→FPP chain extension.

evidence.title: Metabolic Engineering Escherichia coli for the Production of Lycopene
evidence.authors: Wang Z, Sun J, Yang Q, Yang J
evidence.evidence_sentence: isopentenyl pyrophosphate (IPP), dimethylallyl pyrophosphate (DMAPP) and farnesyl pyrophosphate (FPP) are synthesized by 8 sequential enzymes in the MEP pathway, after which they are converted to lycopene
evidence.claim: DMAPP is required for FPP formation (CrtE/IspA lineage)

edges.mechanism: IspB (octaprenyl-PP synthase) is the essential competing FPP sink producing ubiquinone/menaquinone.

evidence.title: Metabolic Engineering Escherichia coli for the Production of Lycopene
evidence.authors: Wang Z, Sun J, Yang Q, Yang J
evidence.evidence_sentence: FPP, in turn, is catalyzed by geranylgeranyl diphosphate (GGPP) synthase (CrtE)
evidence.claim: IspB competes with CrtE for FPP; modulation of ispB affects carotenoid flux

edges.mechanism: GPP is a product-inhibitor of MVK (Chen 2022); caps MVA downstream flux.

evidence.title: Directed evolution of mevalonate kinase in E. coli by random mutagenesis for improved lycopene
evidence.authors: Chen H, Liu C, Li M, Zhang H, Xian M, Liu H
evidence.evidence_sentence: MKs are subject to feedback inhibition by lycopene biosynthesis intermediates, such as geranyldiphosphate (GPP), farnesyldiphosphate (FPP), and geranylgeranyldiphosphate (GGPP)
evidence.claim: GPP, FPP, GGPP feedback-inhibit MVK, throttling upstream MVA

edges.mechanism: FPP feedback-inhibits MVK in the same allosteric site.

evidence.title: Directed evolution of mevalonate kinase in E. coli by random mutagenesis for improved lycopene
evidence.authors: Chen H, Liu C, Li M, Zhang H, Xian M, Liu H
evidence.evidence_sentence: MKs are subject to feedback inhibition by lycopene biosynthesis intermediates, such as geranyldiphosphate (GPP), farnesyldiphosphate (FPP), and geranylgeranyldiphosphate (GGPP)
evidence.claim: GPP, FPP, GGPP feedback-inhibit MVK, throttling upstream MVA

edges.mechanism: GGPP feedback-inhibits MVK, closing a tight three-level product loop.

evidence.title: Directed evolution of mevalonate kinase in E. coli by random mutagenesis for improved lycopene
evidence.authors: Chen H, Liu C, Li M, Zhang H, Xian M, Liu H
evidence.evidence_sentence: MKs are subject to feedback inhibition by lycopene biosynthesis intermediates, such as geranyldiphosphate (GPP), farnesyldiphosphate (FPP), and geranylgeranyldiphosphate (GGPP)
evidence.claim: GPP, FPP, GGPP feedback-inhibit MVK, throttling upstream MVA

edges.mechanism: Heterologous CrtE condenses FPP + IPP to GGPP.

evidence.title: Advances in the Discovery and Engineering of Gene Targets for Carotenoid Biosynthesis in Recombinant Strains
evidence.authors: Su B, Deng MR, Zhu H
evidence.evidence_sentence: transformation of DMAPP into GGPP...was recognized as the pivotal step that restricts the rate of carotenoid biosynthesis...enhanced the expression of CrtE...60% increase in lycopene production
evidence.claim: CrtE/GGPP synthase is rate-limiting for carotenoid biosynthesis

evidence.title: Engineering the lycopene synthetic pathway in E. coli by comparison of the carotenoid genes of Pantoea agglomerans and Pantoea ananatis
evidence.authors: Lee JH, Kim SW, Lee PC
evidence.evidence_sentence: The crtE gene was responsible for the observed difference in lycopene production and cell growth between E. coli harboring the crt genes of P. agglomerans and P. ananatis
evidence.claim: CrtE enzymatic activity determines lycopene yield across source organisms

edges.mechanism: FPP substrate pool feeds GGPP synthesis.

evidence.title: Metabolic Engineering Escherichia coli for the Production of Lycopene
evidence.authors: Wang Z, Sun J, Yang Q, Yang J
evidence.evidence_sentence: FPP, in turn, is catalyzed by geranylgeranyl diphosphate (GGPP) synthase (CrtE)
evidence.claim: FPP is substrate for GGPP formation

edges.mechanism: Heterologous CrtB condenses two GGPP into phytoene (C40).

evidence.title: Metabolic Engineering Escherichia coli for the Production of Lycopene
evidence.authors: Wang Z, Sun J, Yang Q, Yang J
evidence.evidence_sentence: phytoene synthase (CrtB)...successively form...phytoene
evidence.claim: CrtB catalyses GGPP->phytoene

edges.mechanism: GGPP substrate pool feeds phytoene synthesis.

evidence.title: Metabolic Engineering Escherichia coli for the Production of Lycopene
evidence.authors: Wang Z, Sun J, Yang Q, Yang J
evidence.evidence_sentence: FPP is then catalyzed by geranylgeranyl diphosphate synthase (CrtE), phytoene synthase (CrtB), and phytoene desaturase (CrtI) to successively form GGPP, phytoene, and finally lycopene
evidence.claim: GGPP is substrate for phytoene

edges.mechanism: Heterologous CrtI executes the four-step desaturation producing lycopene. Terminal productive enzyme.

evidence.title: Metabolic Engineering Escherichia coli for the Production of Lycopene
evidence.authors: Wang Z, Sun J, Yang Q, Yang J
evidence.evidence_sentence: phytoene desaturase (CrtI) to successively form...finally lycopene
evidence.claim: CrtI directly converts phytoene to lycopene

evidence.title: Advances in the Discovery and Engineering of Gene Targets for Carotenoid Biosynthesis in Recombinant Strains
evidence.authors: Su B, Deng MR, Zhu H
evidence.evidence_sentence: produced an engineered CrtI mutant (Y160F&N576S)...60% increase in lycopene production
evidence.claim: CrtI variant improves lycopene titer via directed evolution

edges.mechanism: Phytoene substrate pool feeds the CrtI-driven lycopene step (pool contribution).

evidence.title: Metabolic Engineering Escherichia coli for the Production of Lycopene
evidence.authors: Wang Z, Sun J, Yang Q, Yang J
evidence.evidence_sentence: phytoene desaturase (CrtI) to successively form...finally lycopene
evidence.claim: Phytoene->Lycopene by CrtI

edges.mechanism: CrtI requires NADPH (FADH2 via regeneration) for its four desaturation steps.

evidence.title: Metabolic Engineering Escherichia coli for the Production of Lycopene
evidence.authors: Wang Z, Sun J, Yang Q, Yang J
evidence.evidence_sentence: IPP and DMAPP...need NADPH and ATP
evidence.claim: NADPH supply supports CrtI desaturations

edges.mechanism: GPP accumulation at high MVA OE is toxic to the host, lowering lycopene titer (Cheng 2022).

evidence.title: Fine-tuning the downstream MVA pathway by systematic RBS engineering for improved lycopene production
evidence.authors: Cheng T, Wang L, Sun C, Xie C
evidence.evidence_sentence: Excessive midstream module flux harmed lycopene accumulation and cell growth, which could be due to the downstream module's limited capacity or the potential cytotoxicity of accumulated intermediate metabolites like GPP and FPP
evidence.claim: Over-expression of MVA midstream -> GPP/FPP accumulation toxicity -> reduced growth and titer

edges.mechanism: FPP accumulation toxicity (Chen 2022) lowers titer when downstream CrtE is rate-limiting.

evidence.title: Fine-tuning the downstream MVA pathway by systematic RBS engineering for improved lycopene production
evidence.authors: Cheng T, Wang L, Sun C, Xie C
evidence.evidence_sentence: Excessive midstream module flux harmed lycopene accumulation and cell growth, which could be due to the downstream module's limited capacity or the potential cytotoxicity of accumulated intermediate metabolites like GPP and FPP
evidence.claim: Over-expression of MVA midstream -> GPP/FPP accumulation toxicity -> reduced growth and titer

edges.mechanism: 37 degC vs 30 degC reduces soluble heterologous enzyme and pigment stability.

evidence.title: Metabolic Engineering Escherichia coli for the Production of Lycopene
evidence.authors: Wang Z, Sun J, Yang Q, Yang J
evidence.evidence_sentence: temperature-shift culture method (37 to 25 degC)...further augment lycopene production
evidence.claim: Higher T reduces lycopene titer

edges.mechanism: Sigma-S activity (via KatE and other stationary-phase targets) contributes to accumulated lycopene (Alper 2005).

evidence.title: Systems analysis of methylerythritol-phosphate pathway flux in E. coli: insights into the role of oxidative stress
evidence.authors: Bongers JG, et al.
evidence.evidence_sentence: knockout of rpoS does not influence flux through the MEP pathway...increased accumulation of reactive oxygen species during late log and stationary phases
evidence.claim: RpoS protects lycopene from ROS degradation via KatE induction

evidence.title: Metabolic Engineering Escherichia coli for the Production of Lycopene
evidence.authors: Wang Z, Sun J, Yang Q, Yang J
evidence.evidence_sentence: RpoS could help accumulate lycopene by decreasing oxidative stress in the growth stationary phase
evidence.claim: RpoS supports lycopene accumulation via ROS defense

edges.mechanism: RpoS transcriptionally induces katE (HP-II catalase) in stationary phase.

evidence.title: Systems analysis of methylerythritol-phosphate pathway flux in E. coli: insights into the role of oxidative stress
evidence.authors: Bongers JG, et al.
evidence.evidence_sentence: concentration of the stationary phase-specific catalase KatE...was reduced >twofold in MG1655*
evidence.claim: KatE is RpoS-dependent stationary-phase catalase

edges.mechanism: Crl post-translationally stabilises RpoS-holoenzyme assembly.

evidence.title: Metabolic Engineering Escherichia coli for the Production of Lycopene
evidence.authors: Wang Z, Sun J, Yang Q, Yang J
evidence.evidence_sentence: Crl (transcriptional regulator of csgBA for curli surface fiber formation) also enhanced lycopene production
evidence.claim: Crl promotes RpoS activity and enhances lycopene accumulation

edges.mechanism: Hnr (RssB) adaptor targets RpoS for ClpXP degradation; Δhnr raises RpoS and lycopene.

evidence.title: Metabolic Engineering Escherichia coli for the Production of Lycopene
evidence.authors: Wang Z, Sun J, Yang Q, Yang J
evidence.evidence_sentence: Î”hnr Î”yliE mutant...significantly improved lycopene production
evidence.claim: Î”hnr stabilises RpoS and boosts lycopene

edges.mechanism: Oxidised [2Fe-2S] SoxR activates soxS transcription.

evidence.title: Metabolic Engineering Escherichia coli for the Production of Lycopene
evidence.authors: Wang Z, Sun J, Yang Q, Yang J
evidence.evidence_sentence: The SoxRS regulon is a response mechanism to O2- and to redox-cycling compounds, but not to H2O2
evidence.claim: SoxR activates soxS in response to superoxide

edges.mechanism: SoxS upregulates zwf for NADPH demand during oxidative stress.

evidence.title: Metabolic Engineering Escherichia coli for the Production of Lycopene
evidence.authors: Wang Z, Sun J, Yang Q, Yang J
evidence.evidence_sentence: Expression of zwf gene that encodes the first key enzyme from the pentose phosphate pathway is among others controlled by the SoxRS regulon in response to oxidative stress
evidence.claim: SoxS transcriptionally activates zwf

edges.mechanism: KatE protects lycopene from peroxide-mediated oxidative degradation; KatE OE collapses strain-level titer variation (Bongers 2015).

evidence.title: Systems analysis of methylerythritol-phosphate pathway flux in E. coli: oxidative stress and lycopene as reporter
evidence.authors: Bongers JG, Mol R, Mastrangelo N, de Kok JD, Stephanopoulos G, Vickers CE
evidence.evidence_sentence: katE was decreased twofold in MG1655* and overexpression of katE increased measurable lycopene in MG1655*
evidence.claim: KatE (HPII catalase) OE protects lycopene from H2O2-mediated oxidative degradation and elevates measured titer

edges.mechanism: Catalase KatE decomposes hydrogen peroxide.

evidence.title: Systems analysis of methylerythritol-phosphate pathway flux in E. coli: insights into the role of oxidative stress
evidence.authors: Bongers JG, et al.
evidence.evidence_sentence: overexpression of katE increased measurable lycopene in MG1655*, reducing the difference...to less than two-fold from previously eightfold
evidence.claim: KatE decomposes H2O2 and protects lycopene from oxidative degradation

edges.mechanism: Lycopene is the most efficient singlet-oxygen quencher known; accumulating pigment reduces cellular ROS (kq ~3×10^10 M^-1 s^-1).

evidence.title: Systems analysis of methylerythritol-phosphate pathway flux in E. coli: oxidative stress and lycopene as reporter
evidence.authors: Bongers JG, Mol R, Mastrangelo N, de Kok JD, Stephanopoulos G, Vickers CE
evidence.evidence_sentence: Lycopene is a potent antioxidant... The chromogenic conjugated double bonds of lycopene are readily oxidized, leading to loss of the characteristic red color
evidence.claim: Lycopene quenches ROS and is consumed in the process; accumulated lycopene is partially degraded by H2O2 and superoxide

edges.mechanism: Reactive oxygen species oxidatively degrade lycopene's polyene backbone — the dominant explanation of strain-level titer variation (Bongers 2015).

evidence.title: Systems analysis of methylerythritol-phosphate pathway flux in E. coli: insights into the role of oxidative stress
evidence.authors: Bongers JG, et al.
evidence.evidence_sentence: measurable lycopene content decreased significantly in the presence of sublethal doses of menadione
evidence.claim: ROS chemically degrade lycopene in vivo

evidence.title: Systems analysis of methylerythritol-phosphate pathway flux in E. coli: oxidative stress and lycopene as reporter
evidence.authors: Bongers JG, Mol R, Mastrangelo N, de Kok JD, Stephanopoulos G, Vickers CE
evidence.evidence_sentence: Lycopene is a potent antioxidant... The chromogenic conjugated double bonds of lycopene are readily oxidized, leading to loss of the characteristic red color
evidence.claim: Lycopene quenches ROS and is consumed in the process; accumulated lycopene is partially degraded by H2O2 and superoxide

evidence.title: Systems analysis of methylerythritol-phosphate pathway flux in E. coli: oxidative stress and lycopene as reporter
evidence.authors: Bongers JG, Mol R, Mastrangelo N, de Kok JD, Stephanopoulos G, Vickers CE
evidence.evidence_sentence: knockout of rpoS does not influence flux through the MEP pathway... RpoS has no effect on the abundance of any of the enzymes involved in lycopene biosynthesis
evidence.claim: Bongers 2015 finds the Alper 2005 RpoS effect is ROS-driven lycopene loss, not MEP flux

edges.mechanism: Acholeplasma Almgs synergises with PlsB to expand membrane phospholipid reservoir (Hu 2018).

evidence.title: Engineering membrane morphology and manipulating synthesis for increased lycopene accumulation in E. coli
evidence.authors: Hu G, Chen X, Wu J, Liu L
evidence.evidence_sentence: The strain LYC101-37Almgs(pPlsb-plsc) had a more pronounced increase of lycopene production which reached 216.3 mg/L lycopene with a specific production of 37.7 mg/g DCW at 48 h, and 358.9 mg/L lycopene with 36.4 mg/g DCW at 96 h
evidence.claim: Synergy between Almgs and PlsB+PlsC: 1.32-fold increase in specific lycopene vs parent

evidence.title: Engineering membrane morphology and manipulating synthesis for increased lycopene accumulation in E. coli
evidence.authors: Hu G, Chen X, Wu J, Liu L
evidence.evidence_sentence: In the engineered strain LYC101-37Almgs(pPlsb-plsc), the membrane-associated lycopene fraction increased markedly, reaching to 73.2% of the total content
evidence.claim: Membrane capacity limits accumulation -- expanded membrane reservoir -> higher retained titer (73.2% vs 55.4% membrane fraction)

edges.mechanism: PlsB (G3P acyltransferase) expands the phospholipid membrane pool, increasing hydrophobic lycopene storage capacity.

evidence.title: Metabolic Engineering Escherichia coli for the Production of Lycopene
evidence.authors: Wang Z, Sun J, Yang Q, Yang J
evidence.evidence_sentence: Plsb (glycerol-3-phosphate acyltransferase) and Plsc...were overexpressed...cells produced sufficient intracellular membrane vesicles
evidence.claim: Plsb OE expands membrane capacity for lycopene storage
